instruction: Questions 21–24: Choose A, B or C. Questions 25–28: Matching A–F. Questions 29–30: Choose A, B or C.

questions.prompt: At first, Don thought the topic of recycling footwear might be too

options.label: limited in scope.
hard to research.
boring for listeners.

questions.sectionInstruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.prompt: When discussing trainers, Bella and Don disagree about

options.label: how popular they are among young people.
how suitable they are for school.
how quickly they wear out.

questions.prompt: Bella says that she sometimes recycles shoes because

options.label: they no longer fit.
she no longer likes them.
they are no longer in fashion.

questions.prompt: What did the article say that confused Don?

options.label: Public consumption of footwear has risen.
Less footwear is recycled now than in the past.
People dispose of more footwear than they used to.
one shoe was missing
the colour of one shoe had faded
one shoe had a hole in it
the shoes were brand new
the shoes were too dirty
the stitching on the shoes was broken

questions.prompt: Why did the project to make 'new' shoes out of old shoes fail?

options.label: People believed the 'new' pairs of shoes were unhygienic.
There were not enough good parts to use in the old shoes.
The shoes in the 'new' pairs were not completely alike.

options.label: from a new angle.
